best_agent_match.title: Native-balance based success can be spoofed with third-party ETH donations

findings.title: No end-to-end profit check lets losing executions complete successfully

best_agent_match.title: Native-balance based success can be spoofed with third-party ETH donations

rejected_candidates.title: Native-balance based success can be spoofed with third-party ETH donations
rejected_candidates.reason: `executeOnOpportunity()` performs no native-balance-based success check at all, and the only in-function profitability gate in `_tryCycle()` is based on WETH balance, not ETH donations. Without an on-chain or provided harness check, this remains too speculative.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy guard exposes the treasury to callback-driven recursive execution
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract lacks a reentrancy guard, but the report does not identify a concrete callback-capable path among the fixed hard-coded counterparties that can realistically reenter `executeOnOpportunity()`. As written, the claim is too speculative for a reportable issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is fully mutable by any external account
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter.sol` is a trivial sample contract with no privileged state, funds, or security-sensitive integration shown in scope. Unrestricted mutation of its lone counter variable is expected behavior, not a realistic protocol-impact vulnerability.
